description_long: This is a small, single-room chapel with a simple rectangular footprint and a steeply pitched, weathered tile roof. The front facade features a wooden door and a prominent stone cross above the entrance, set into a plain whitewashed wall. The side walls are unadorned except for a single rectangular window on one side and a wooden-shuttered opening on the other, both framed simply. The rear wall has a small, square, wooden-shuttered window near the roofline. The roof is covered with reddish-brown tiles, showing signs of age and moss, and a single chimney rises from one side. The chapel sits on a grassy base, suggesting it is freestanding and likely found in a quiet urban or suburban setting.
description: A small, whitewashed chapel with a steep tiled roof, wooden door, and a stone cross above the entrance. It features simple windows and a chimney, set on a grassy base.
description_view_0: Front view showing the wooden entrance door, stone cross above the door, and the plain white facade.
description_view_1: Side view displaying a single rectangular window with a wooden frame and the full length of the tiled roof and chimney.
description_view_2: Rear view with a small, square, wooden-shuttered window near the roofline and a plain white wall.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing a closed, rectangular wooden-shuttered window and the weathered roof tiles.
attribute_architectural_style: Vernacular European, with elements of Rural or Folk architecture
attribute_entrance_door_design: The main entrance door is rectangular, made of weathered wood, and has a simple, rustic appearance with no visible decorative carvings or embellishments. It is set into a plain white wall beneath a stone cross.
